name: hass-mcp
title: Home Assistant (community)
description: Home Assistant operations via REST API — entity state, history, areas, services, automations, camera snapshots, energy monitoring
tagline: Community Home Assistant with camera snapshots
author: bendavid

highlights.text: Entity state — query entities by domain, area, or device class
History — state change history and long-term statistics
Camera snapshots — capture and retrieve camera images
Energy — solar, grid, and device-level energy monitoring
Automations — list, trigger, and manage automation rules
Multi-instance — connect to multiple HA instances

readme:
### What it does

Hass MCP is a community-maintained Home Assistant plugin that provides entity state queries, history lookups, device control, automation management, camera snapshots, and energy monitoring through the HA REST API.

It covers the core Home Assistant operations most users need: checking device states, reviewing state history, triggering automations, calling services, and capturing camera images.

### Community plugin

This is a verified community plugin maintained by bendavid. It implements the `home-v1` service contract and is interchangeable with the Home Assistant Blade for users who prefer the community version. It passes conformance testing on all required and recommended operations, with automation creation being the only missing gated capability.

### Comparison with Home Assistant Blade

| Feature | Hass MCP | HA Blade |
|---------|----------|---------|
| Maintainer | Community (bendavid) | Sidereal (certified) |
| Camera snapshots | Yes | No |
| Multi-site | Yes | Yes (native providers) |
| Automation create | No | Yes |
| Write gating | Not gated | Explicit write gate |
| Token efficiency | Standard | Concise formatters |

### Setup

Setup is straightforward: provide the Home Assistant URL and a Long-Lived Access Token created from Profile > Security in your HA instance. Multi-instance support works by running multiple plugin instances with different URL/token pairs.

env.name: HA_TOKEN
env.description: Long-lived access token from Home Assistant

setup.blurb: Connect to your Home Assistant instance for device monitoring, automation control, and energy tracking.
setup.help_text: Create a Long-Lived Access Token at Profile → Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens in your Home Assistant instance.

fields.label: Home Assistant URL
fields.help: The base URL of your Home Assistant instance.

scenarios.label: Morning home status check
scenarios.body: Query all entity states, summarise which devices are on, check overnight security events, and report any sensors in an abnormal state.

scenarios.label: Review camera activity
scenarios.body: List camera entities, capture current snapshots from each, and compare with historical state to identify any motion events during overnight hours.

scenarios.label: Track solar production vs grid usage
scenarios.body: Pull energy statistics for the past day, compare solar production against grid consumption, and identify peak usage periods where battery or load-shifting could help.
